skill.name: ctd-api
skill.description: Accesses Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD) for chemical, gene, disease, and pathway interaction data. Invoke when user needs to query CTD, retrieve toxicogenomics data, or investigate chemical-disease relationships.
skill.summary: Accesses Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD) for chemical, gene, disease, and pathway interaction data. Invoke when user needs to query CTD, retrieve toxicogenomics data, or investigate chemical-disease relationships.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found 119 potential issues, but all HIGH and MEDIUM severity findings (Weak cryptographic algorithm, Ruby/shell backtick execution) are FALSE POSITIVES caused by the scanner misinterpreting Markdown code formatting and JSON data as executable code or cryptographic operations. The LOW severity findings (network/hardcoded URLs) are TRUE POSITIVES but legitimate: the script connects to the official CTD scientific database at ctdbase.org. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, credential theft, or command injection was found after thorough review.

low_findings.title: Network requests to external API
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to ctdbase.org using urllib.request. This is the core functionality of the skill and the target is a legitimate scientific database. No data exfiltration or suspicious endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API URL
low_findings.description: The CTD API base URL is hardcoded in the script. This is expected behavior for a skill that queries a fixed public API endpoint. The URL points to the official CTD database at ctdbase.org.

content.value_statement: Researchers struggle to efficiently access curated chemical-gene-disease interaction data from the CTD database. This skill automates batch queries to the CTD API, enabling rapid retrieval of toxicogenomics associations without manual web browsing.
actual_capabilities: Query curated and inferred associations between chemicals, genes, diseases, pathways, and phenotypes using the CTD Batch Query API
Support multiple input types including chemical, gene, disease, GO term, pathway, phenotype, and reference identifiers
Retrieve results in JSON, TSV, CSV, or XML formats for downstream analysis
Handle network errors with automatic retry logic and structured error messages
Generate direct URLs to CTD entity detail pages for chemicals, diseases, genes, GO terms, and pathways
limitations: Requires internet access to connect to the CTD API at ctdbase.org
Cannot authenticate or query private databases, only the public CTD API
Does not perform multi-step analysis or generate scientific conclusions from the data
Limited to the input types and report type combinations documented by the CTD API

use_cases.title: Chemical toxicology screening
use_cases.description: Screen chemicals against known disease and gene interaction data to identify potential toxicological risks for environmental health research.

use_cases.title: Gene-disease association discovery
use_cases.description: Explore which diseases are linked to specific genes using curated CTD data for biomedical research and hypothesis generation.

use_cases.title: Pathway and phenotype analysis
use_cases.description: Investigate biological pathways and phenotypes associated with chemicals or diseases to understand molecular mechanisms of toxicity.

prompt_templates.title: Basic chemical query
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the Comparative Toxicogenomics Database for all curated genes associated with Mercury. Use inputType 'chem' and report 'genes_curated' with JSON output.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher wants to find known gene interactions for a specific chemical.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-gene disease association
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve curated disease associations for genes BRCA1 and TP53 from the CTD. Use inputType 'gene' with inputTerms 'BRCA1' 'TP53' and report 'diseases_curated'.
prompt_templates.scenario: A geneticist wants to explore which diseases have curated associations with specific genes.

prompt_templates.title: Chemical pathway enrichment
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for enriched pathway associations for the chemical Bisphenol A using the CTD API. Use inputType 'chem' with report 'pathways_enriched' and format 'json'.
prompt_templates.scenario: A toxicologist needs to identify biological pathways affected by a specific chemical exposure.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive cross-reference analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a comprehensive CTD analysis for the chemical 'Bisphenol A'. Run queries for curated genes, curated diseases, enriched pathways, and curated phenotypes. Compare results across all report types and summarize the strongest associations.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher conducting a systematic review needs to compile all available CTD data for a chemical of concern.

output_examples.input: Find curated diseases associated with the chemical Mercury
output: Curated Disease Associations: Alzheimer Disease, Autism Spectrum Disorder,
Cardiovascular Diseases, Kidney Diseases, Nervous System Diseases

output_examples.input: Query curated chemical associations for asthma
output: Curated Chemical Associations: Acetaminophen, Allergens, Ammonia,
Asbestos, Cadmium, Diesel Exhaust, Formaldehyde, Ozone, Particulate Matter, Tobacco Smoke

best_practices: Verify that input terms are valid MeSH IDs or NCBI Gene IDs for the most accurate and complete results.
Specify the output format explicitly to match your downstream processing pipeline and avoid parsing issues.
Start with curated reports before using inferred data to ensure higher confidence in the results.
anti_patterns: Do not treat CTD query results as clinical diagnostic evidence or medical advice; CTD is a research database.
Avoid querying with overly broad or unvalidated terms without identifier verification as this may return incomplete results.
Do not assume all CTD data is complete or up to date; annotations may have gaps and can change over time.

faq.question: What is the Comparative Toxicogenomics Database?
faq.answer: CTD is a public scientific database that provides curated and inferred associations between chemicals, genes, diseases, pathways, and phenotypes to support research on environmental health and toxicogenomics.

faq.question: What types of data can I query with this skill?
faq.answer: You can query chemicals, diseases, genes, GO terms, pathways, phenotypes, and references. Each input type supports different report types such as curated associations, inferred associations, and enriched pathways.

faq.question: Do I need an API key or authentication to use CTD?
faq.answer: No. The CTD Batch Query API is publicly accessible without authentication or API keys. Simply provide valid input terms and a report type.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON, TSV, CSV, and XML output formats. JSON is the default format and is recommended for automated processing.

faq.question: How do I find valid identifiers for my query?
faq.answer: Use standard MeSH IDs for chemicals and diseases, NCBI Gene IDs for genes, and standard GO identifiers for GO terms. Common chemical or gene names may also work in many cases.

faq.question: What happens if my query returns no results?
faq.answer: The script returns a structured error message indicating the issue. This may mean no associations exist for your terms, or the identifiers may be incorrect. Try verifying your input terms and checking the CTD website.
